title: Cozy Nutty Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A comforting lowcarb lamb tagine infused with cinnamon, almonds, and sweet apricots for a nutty, aromatic finish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a Dutch‑oven over medium heat and brown the lamb cubes on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add the sliced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until the onion is translucent.
Stir in cinnamon, cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot, pour in the chicken broth, and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 60 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chopped apricots and toasted almonds, then continue to simmer, uncovered, for another 20 minutes until the lamb is tender and the sauce thickens.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Stir in a splash of lemon juice if desired for a bright contrast.
Serve the tagine hot, either over cauliflower rice or with a side of roasted vegetables.
Garnish with fresh herbs if desired and enjoy a cozy, nutty Moroccan feast.
